channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Roster limit changes not offered in latest filing on $2.8 billion NCAA lawsuit settlement
item.description: Attorneys working on the legal settlement designed to reshape college sports filed a brief that did not offer changes a judge suggested regarding roster limits, reiterating the benefit the limits provide to some athletes and the havoc a late change to that rule could create.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Paul Skenes and Henry Davis, taken 1st overall two years apart, make MLB history
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — From the moment they heard their names called out by Major League Baseball commissioner Rob Manfred, Paul Skenes and Henry Davis were destined to make history.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Young Bulls, Lonzo Ball and future of his highly-touted twin sons with Carlos Boozer
item.description: Carlos Boozer recently spoke with Jarrett Payton to discuss the Chicago Bulls' young team, Lonzo Ball's injury, and his sons' basketball success, with both Cameron and Cayden Boozer being highly-touted high school recruits and planning to attend Duke University.
item.dc:creator: Jarrett Payton, WGN 9

item.title: Paige Bueckers is No. 1 pick in WNBA draft, going to the Dallas Wings
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Paige Bueckers is ready for her next chapter after a whirlwind week that started with her helping UConn win its 12th national championship and ended with her becoming the WNBA's No. 1 draft pick by the Dallas Wings. “I’m just extremely excited to be there.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Colorado will retire Shedeur Sanders’ No. 2 and Travis Hunter's No. 12 in ceremony at spring game
item.description: Shedeur Sanders and Travis Hunter left big footprints in only two seasons on the field at Colorado. For that, their numbers will be retired Saturday at the spring game as part of the afternoon's festivities. Sanders’ No. 2 and Hunter’s No.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Injury-prone Rockies DH Kris Bryant heads to IL for 9th time since joining the team in 2022
item.description: Colorado Rockies designated hitter Kris Bryant has landed on the injured list with a bad back, his ninth IL stint since joining the team.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hailey Van Lith turns heads on WNBA Draft red carpet
item.description: Hailey Van Lith stunned on the WNBA Draft red carpet on Monday night before she heard her name called and she officially turned pro in New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sharks captain Logan Couture is expected to announce the end of his career because of an injury
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — San Jose Sharks captain Logan Couture is expected to announce he will end his playing career because of a debilitating injury that has sidelined him for more than 15 months.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jazz reflect on tough season, look to the future
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz finished the 2024-25 season not with just the worst record in the NBA, but the worst in franchise history. At 17-65, Jazz fans are extremely frustrated, but the Jazz brass insist brighter days are ahead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Crochet is still Crochet, even if Red now precedes 'Sox' on the front of his jersey
item.description: Garrett Crochet, the former White Sox pitcher, made his debut for the Boston Red Sox on Sunday, shutting down the Chicago White Sox with 11 strikeouts and one run. Despite the trade, Crochet has expressed his fondness for his former team and the memories he made on Chicago's South Side.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Why Harper, Schwarber, Turner believe Posey will excel as SF Giants’ team president
item.description: Posey faced Bryce Harper, Kyle Schwarber and Trea Turner's teams in the playoffs during his playing career for the Giants.
item.dc:creator: Justice delos Santos, The Mercury News

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman remembered for his love of family, faith and cheeseburgers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — George Foreman was remembered Monday in a memorial service in his hometown of Houston for his legendary boxing career as well as for his love of God, family, horses and cheeseburgers and for his desire to help his fellow man. “He preached love all the time.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawks' Risacher enters play-in as the last man standing from French players to top last 2 NBA drafts
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Atlanta Hawks rookie Zaccharie Risacher is the lone survivor of the influx of French players who have dominated the top of the last two NBA drafts. Risacher and the Hawks will play at Orlando on Tuesday night in the NBA play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Phoenix Suns fire coach Mike Budenholzer after one dismal season with high-priced roster
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Phoenix Suns have parted ways with veteran coach Mike Budenholzer following one dismal season that featured a fast start before a maddening slide out of postseason contention for a high-priced roster that included Kevin Durant, Devin Booker and Bradley Beal.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kyle Larson and Denny Hamlin renew rivalry at Bristol with their seventh 1-2 finish
item.description: A second consecutive victory at Bristol Motor Speedway was especially sweet for Kyle Larson, and one reason loomed large in his rearview mirror.
item.dc:creator: NATE RYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rory McIlroy has masterful start at Augusta and closes in on career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The greatest start in the 91-year history of the Masters wasn't enough for Rory McIlroy. He delivered what looked to be a haymaker late Saturday afternoon, a 6-iron so majestic and pure that he walked 10 yards before he saw it land 6 feet away for eagle on the 15th hole.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blackhawks score 4 3rd-period goals for 5-2 win over Bruins
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Nick Foligno scored a pair of goals, Arvid Soderblom made 31 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Boston Bruins 5-2 on Thursday night. Ryan Donato, Tyler Bertuzzi and Kevin Korchinski also had goals for the Blackhawks, who scored four goals in third period to pull away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nolan Jones draws walk with bases-loaded to give Guardians 1-0 victory over White Sox in home opener
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Nolan Jones drew a bases-loaded walk from Mike Clevinger in the ninth inning, and the Cleveland Guardians won their home opener 1-0 over the Chicago White Sox on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball: Canyon Hills 10, Southwest 7
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great Monday to play some baseball with Southwest hosting Canyon Hills. Rattlers go on to win a score fest 10-7. The Rattlers scored six in the first and held on to win, despite Southwest cutting it down to 9-7 before tacking on one more run to win by three.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Loyola University Chicago sent preservation notice tied to federally indicted ex-Michigan coach
item.description: Loyola University Chicago has received a preservation notice from attorneys representing a former student-athlete, compelling the school to preserve evidence related to the indictment of former Michigan football assistant coach Matt Weiss on unauthorized computer access and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
